---agent: 'agent'name: 'create-component'description: 'Implement a component to be used on gameobjects.'---# TaskImplement a Component to realize the behaviour described by the user.# Context### What is a component?A component is a class in the component oriented architecture, that implements a discreet piece of behaviour. These are then attached to `GameObject`-s, on which their behaviour acts. Such components are for example `MouseFollowerComponent` or `SpriteRenderComponent`.### What is a gameobject?A simple container for a `Transform`-- a container for position, rotation and size-- and a list of components.### Game systemsOverarching behaviours are implemented inside systems. These systems are stored inside the `Game` class as interface references. Such systems are the rendering in #file:IRenderer.h, or the input system in #file:IInputService.h.currently available systems are:- IPhysicsEngine- IRenderer- IInputService- ILogger# InstructionsWhen implementing a component, follow this workflow: - Think through what the user wants to implement.- Collect some context from the systems you are about to use.- Take a deep breath. How could you implement this component using these systems cleanly? Can you even? Maybe you should ask the user for further instructions.- Declare the component class in a new header file, placed in the appropriate folder.- Declare the methods in its interfaces.- Implement the declared methods in a new source file, placed in the appropriate folder.- Test if the code compiles. If you introduced some errors, fix them.- Implement the component parsing in the `SceneLoader` class.- Test if the code compiles again. If it does, then you are done.# Further instructions### The component classEvery component inherits from the `Component` abstract class. If multiple components share a similar behaviour, then they inherit through a subclass. For now, the only such subclass is `RenderComponent` .Components use the various systems to implement their behaviour. These systems are passed to them as references through dependency injection.Here is an example for a component class:input/MouseFollowerComponent.h:```#include "core/GameObject.h"#include "graphics/IViewport.h"#include "input/IInputService.h"#include "core/Component.h"#include <string>class MouseFollowerComponent final : public Component{public:	MouseFollowerComponent(GameObject* owner, const IViewport& camera, IInputService& inputService) :		Component(owner), viewPort(camera), InputService(inputService)	{}	~MouseFollowerComponent() override = default;	void OnAdd() override;	void Update() override;	void OnRemove() override;	std::string ToString() const override;private:	const IViewport& viewPort;	IInputService& InputService;};```input/MouseFollowerComponent.cpp:```#include "input/MouseClickLoggerComponent.h"void MouseClickLoggerComponent::onNotify(Event* event){	logger.Log(std::format("[MouseClickLoggerComponent] Mouse click event received at position ({}, {})",		inputService.GetMousePosition().x,		inputService.GetMousePosition().y	), LogLevel::INFO);}void MouseClickLoggerComponent::OnAdd(){	inputService.mouseClickSubject.subscribe(this);}void MouseClickLoggerComponent::OnRemove(){	inputService.mouseClickSubject.unsubscribe(this);}std::string MouseClickLoggerComponent::ToString() const{	return "<MouseClickLoggerComponent>";}```### Appropriate foldersThe project files are sorted into various folders. The header files are in the header folder, the source files are in the src folder. Inside both of these folders are subfolders based on concern. These subfolders are:- core- graphics- input- physics- util### Scene parsingThe game loads gameobjects and components from an XML file. For parsing components, the `SceneLoader` class maintains a map of parsing functions, called `componentFactories`. To enable parsing your component, a new function needs to be added to this map. Implement it directly in SceneLoader::RegisterDefaultComponents(). Here is are a couple examples of such functions:```void SceneLoader::RegisterDefaultComponents(){	// MouseFollowerComponent	componentFactories.try_emplace("MouseFollowerComponent",		[this](GameObject& object, const tx2::XMLElement& /*xmlElem*/)		{			mainGame.Logger.Log(std::format("[SceneLoader] Adding MouseFollowerComponent to {}", object.ToString()), LogLevel::TRACE);			object.EmplaceComponent<MouseFollowerComponent>(mainGame.GetCamera().GetViewport(), mainGame.InputService);		});	// SpriteRenderComponent	componentFactories.try_emplace("SpriteRendererComponent",		[this](GameObject& object, const tx2::XMLElement& xmlElem)		{			auto* spriteIface = dynamic_cast<ISpriteRenderer*>(&mainGame.Renderer);			if (!spriteIface)			{				mainGame.Logger.Log("SpriteRendererComponent: Renderer does not implement ISpriteRenderer", LogLevel::ERROR);				return;			}			SpriteRenderComponentDTO dto;			ParseSpriteRendererXML(xmlElem, dto);			mainGame.Logger.Log(std::format("[SceneLoader] Adding SpriteRendererComponent to {} with texture '{}'", object.ToString(), dto.texturePath), LogLevel::TRACE);			object.EmplaceComponent<SpriteRenderComponent>(mainGame.GetCamera(),													   *spriteIface,													   mainGame.Logger,													   dto);		});…```
